mapping.a_element: Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) with time-dependent scattering length as an analogue model for cosmological particle creation
mapping.b_element: Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) of group field theory (GFT) quanta as a model for emergent continuum spacetime

mapping.a_element: classical field method via the truncated Wigner approximation to simulate the BEC dynamics
mapping.b_element: effective dynamics extracted from the GFT condensate phase to compare with continuum gravity theories

mapping.a_element: time-dependent scattering length in the BEC, mimicking the expansion of a FRW universe
mapping.b_element: phase transition or condensation process in GFT, leading to emergent continuum spacetime

mapping.a_element: particle production in the long wavelength limit, consistent with cosmological theory
mapping.b_element: emergence of continuum spacetime from discrete quantum structures (GFT quanta)

brief_justification: Both papers employ the machinery of Bose-Einstein condensation as a framework to model emergent phenomena: Paper A uses a BEC with a time-dependent scattering length to simulate cosmological particle creation in an expanding universe, while Paper B proposes a BEC of GFT quanta to describe the emergence of continuum spacetime. The underlying method in both is the use of condensate physics to bridge discrete quantum structures and continuum dynamics.
